skippedRootCauseCategories: stage_reflection_error: only affects holdout (p12, p18) — can't target a holdout-only category.
synthesis_issue: only affects holdout (p19) — can't target a holdout-only category.
completeness_check_failure: no current train weak query in this category; deferred.
note: p13 (the only train weak left) regressed under iter 1 because the planner passed placeholder conditionIds ('0xthunder', '0xcavs', '0xknicks') to get_top_holders and the tool returned structured zero-value responses — a false 'no whales' answer. Fix: extended get_top_holders at the contributor boundary to accept slug and marketQuery as alternatives to conditionId, and to auto-resolve via resolveMarketReference when conditionId is missing or doesn't match the 0x+64hex pattern (treating a bogus placeholder as a marketQuery hint rather than a hard fail). If resolution still fails it returns a structured error pointing to search_markets / marketQuery. This is argument_or_extraction_error again but structurally different from iter 1's change (which added positionSizeUsd/side to analyze_market_liquidity); it targets tool-boundary input robustness in get_top_holders specifically. Re-run: p13 now uses analyze_top_holders with marketQuery for each of the three NBA Finals markets and returns real whale concentration data instead of a three-way zero tie. Holdout queries (p12/p18/p19) also completed cleanly with answer outcomes.
